agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement the Order Fulfillment state management system. Bird has defined the domain invariants for order state transitions and refund eligibility. MJ has designed the architecture: a PATCH endpoint for state changes, an OrderStateMachine for transition validation, and a separate RefundService that verifies order state before issuing refunds.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"An order cannot transition from SHIPPED to PENDING","testable_assertion":"Calling transitionState(SHIPPED, PENDING) on OrderStateMachine must throw/return ORDER_STATE_INVALID"},{"rule":"A refund can only be issued on a DELIVERED order","testable_assertion":"RefundService.issueRefund(orderId) must reject with an error when the order state is anything other than DELIVERED"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Expose PATCH /orders/{id}/state for state transitions","implementation_note":"The endpoint receives the target state. Delegate all transition validation to OrderStateMachine before persisting. Return 409 Conflict with structured error body on invalid transitions."},{"decision":"OrderStateMachine class owns all transition validation","implementation_note":"Encapsulate the full set of allowed transitions (e.g., PENDING->SHIPPED, SHIPPED->DELIVERED). This is the single source of truth for what moves are legal. No transition logic should live in controllers or services."},{"decision":"RefundService is a separate service that verifies order state before issuing","implementation_note":"RefundService must call the order service (or query order state) to confirm the order is DELIVERED before processing. Keep refund logic decoupled from state machine logic — RefundService reads state, it does not mutate it."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Invalid state transition is rejected","given":"An order in SHIPPED state","when":"A revert-to-PENDING transition is attempted via PATCH /orders/{id}/state","then":"The request is rejected with error code ORDER_STATE_INVALID and HTTP 409"},{"criterion":"Refund rejected for non-DELIVERED order","given":"An order in any state other than DELIVERED","when":"A refund is requested via RefundService","then":"The refund is rejected with an appropriate error indicating the order is not eligible"},{"criterion":"Refund succeeds for DELIVERED order","given":"An order in DELIVERED state","when":"A refund is requested via RefundService","then":"The refund is processed successfully"},{"criterion":"Valid state transitions succeed","given":"An order in a state with a valid forward transition (e.g., PENDING->SHIPPED, SHIPPED->DELIVERED)","when":"The transition is requested via PATCH /orders/{id}/state","then":"The order state is updated and a 200 OK is returned with the updated order"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Order state invariant","technical_term":"State transition guard in OrderStateMachine","definition":"A business rule that prohibits certain state transitions. Implemented as guard logic that rejects illegal moves before any persistence occurs."},{"domain_term":"ORDER_STATE_INVALID","technical_term":"Error code returned in 409 response body","definition":"The domain error code surfaced when a caller attempts a forbidden state transition. Use this exact string in API error responses."}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":["What is the complete set of valid state transitions? Bird identified SHIPPED->PENDING as invalid and DELIVERED as refund-eligible, but the full state graph (e.g., can DELIVERED->SHIPPED?) needs explicit enumeration before implementation.","Should RefundService call the order service over HTTP or query the order state directly from the database? MJ says 'calls the order service' which implies HTTP — confirm this is intentional vs. an in-process call."]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":82,"high_confidence_areas":["SHIPPED->PENDING rejection rule is unambiguous and both agents agree","Refund-only-on-DELIVERED rule is clear","Separation of OrderStateMachine and RefundService responsibilities"],"low_confidence_areas":["Complete state transition graph beyond the two explicit rules","RefundService integration pattern (HTTP call vs. direct dependency)"],"assumptions":["The bounded context is Order Fulfillment as Bird scoped it — no cross-context concerns surfaced","ORDER_STATE_INVALID is the canonical error code for all invalid transitions, not just SHIPPED->PENDING","The OrderStateMachine is a synchronous, in-process component (not a separate service)"]}}
